props.content: Teaching computers to learn from data
"A program that improves with experience" — Tom Mitchell, 1997
Most real-world ML uses Supervised Learning
The line of best fit minimises error between predictions and real values
→ FORWARD PASS: Data flows left to right
← BACKPROP: Error gradient flows right to left
σ = activation function (e.g. ReLU, Sigmoid, Tanh)
Too large α → overshoot  |  Too small α → slow convergence
Always evaluate on a held-out TEST SET to detect overfitting

ml_pipeline.title: The ML Pipeline — End to End

props.content: 80% of ML time is spent on data collection and preprocessing!
Always split data: 70% Train  |  15% Validation  |  15% Test
Never let test data touch training — that's data leakage!
Start simple. A logistic regression baseline beats no baseline.
